✦ Spark · DSLs in Elixir Contents Cheatsheet
✦ A BookBank field guide

Spark: Building Languages

How to use Elixir's Spark — the framework behind every Ash DSL — to design declarative, self-documenting, deeply introspectable mini-languages. And why that introspectability makes Spark a quietly perfect fit for the LLM-agentic world.

narrated in the voice of Richard Feynman · first-principles, intuition-first

▶ Start reading ⚡ Cheatsheet

Before you turn the page

Here is a funny little idea. Instead of writing a program that does a thing, you write a tiny language in which describing the thing is the whole job — and let the machine turn that description into the working program. That's a DSL, and Spark is the Elixir toolkit that makes building one almost suspiciously pleasant.

Your question pointed at the Spark docs and asked the right thing: not "what is it" in the abstract, but "how do I actually use this, and what exciting things can I build?" So that's the spine of this book. We start from what a DSL even is, build Spark up part by part, and end somewhere genuinely interesting — pointing a large language model at a DSL and letting it read your program's own structure to decide what to do.

🧭
How to read this

Chapters 1–6 build the machine from first principles; you could stop there and go write a real DSL. Chapters 7–8 are the payoff: Spark in an LLM-agentic system, then a complete agent DSL built from scratch.

cover ·

Table of Contents

  1. The Big Idea What a DSL is, why "declarative" wins, and the one sentence that explains all of Spark. the why
  2. Entities, Sections & Extensions The three nested LEGO bricks every Spark DSL is built from. anatomy
  3. Schemas & Spark.Options The little type language that validates your DSL — and writes its own docs. validation
  4. The Compile-Time Pipeline Transformers, persisters, and verifiers — rewriting and checking the DSL as it compiles. compile-time
  5. Introspection & InfoGenerator Getting clean, cached data back out of a DSL at runtime. The secret weapon. runtime
  6. Build Your First DSL A complete data-validator DSL, start to finish — every piece wired together. hands-on
  7. Spark in the Agentic World Why introspectable DSLs are perfect for LLMs — Ash AI's tools do, Jido, and prompt-backed actions. LLM
  8. Design Your Own Agent DSL A from-scratch agent do … tool … end language an LLM can read and call. capstone
cover ·
🔑
About this book

Researched from the official Spark documentation, its source and usage-rules.md, and the Ash ecosystem. Code is verified against Spark 2.7.2 (June 2026) and the modern builder API (Spark.Builder). The agentic chapters draw on Ash AI and Jido, both of which expose their LLM features as Spark DSLs. Everything renders offline.

🍎
A note on the voice

I'll explain everything the way I'd want it explained to me: start from something you already know, build the idea up one honest brick at a time, and never hide behind a fancy word when a plain one will do. When something is genuinely tricky — and a couple of things here are — I'll say so. The joy is in seeing how it works.

Press ⌘R or reopen the book to refresh after edits.
